title: The One with the Fake Monica

jokes.text: l could use another reason why women won't look at me.
jokes.setup: Friends are hesitant to tell him something, making him worry about why someone won't look at him
jokes.punchline: Self-deprecating admission that he already has plenty of reasons women won't look at him
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler self-deprecating humor with perfect economy. Sets up his character's insecurity while getting a laugh.

jokes.text: Excellent dream score.
jokes.setup: Monica admits she had a sex dream about him and Ross
jokes.punchline: He treats it like rating a performance, completely matter-of-fact
jokes.explanation: Chandler's typical deflection through humor, treating an awkward situation with casual commentary.

jokes.text: More importantly... was l any good?
jokes.setup: Monica explains why she had the dream
jokes.punchline: Ross immediately focuses on his performance in someone else's dream
jokes.explanation: Ross's ego and insecurity showing through. Solid character moment but predictable setup.

jokes.text: ln my dreams, l'm surprisingly inadequate.
jokes.setup: Monica says he was 'pretty damn good' in her dream
jokes.punchline: Contrasts with his own dream experiences where he's bad at sex
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor about dream logic and male insecurities. Well-constructed punchline.

jokes.text: Last night, you seemed to know your way around the table.
jokes.setup: Previous discussion about sexual performance in dreams
jokes.punchline: Continues the sexual innuendo while referencing the specific location (table)
jokes.explanation: Extends the uncomfortable sexual discussion. Moderately funny but relies on previous setup.

jokes.text: l love it when we share.
jokes.setup: Ross and Monica continuing to discuss sexual details
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically pretends to enjoy their TMI moment
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler sarcasm cutting through awkwardness. Economical and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Sorry, it was a one-time thing. l was very drunk, and it was someone else's subconscious.
jokes.setup: Joey expressing disbelief about the dream sex
jokes.punchline: Chandler treats dream sex like real cheating, complete with drunk excuse and blame-shifting
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic treating dream infidelity like the real thing. Creative premise with perfect execution and economy.

jokes.text: Ooh, give me. Can you see me operating a drill press?
jokes.setup: Looking at job listings
jokes.punchline: Phoebe excited about operating dangerous machinery despite being completely unsuited for it
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy playing on Phoebe's naive enthusiasm for inappropriate jobs.

jokes.text: l don't know. What are you wearing?
jokes.setup: Phoebe asks if he can see her operating a drill press
jokes.punchline: Chandler treats it like phone sex, focusing on clothing rather than competence
jokes.explanation: Solid misdirection joke with sexual innuendo. Quick and unexpected response.

jokes.text: Pirates again?
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining why she needs work
jokes.punchline: Implies Phoebe has previously lost massage clients to pirates
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly economical callback implying an absurd backstory. Perfect economy of words for maximum confusion and humor.

jokes.text: l taught a 'Massage Yourself at Home' workshop. And they are.
jokes.setup: Explaining why she lost massage clients
jokes.punchline: She taught them to replace her services, making herself obsolete
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe logic - well-intentioned but self-defeating. Good structure with clear setup and payoff.

jokes.text: My secretary's gonna be out. She's having one of her boobs reduced. lt's a whole big boob story.
jokes.setup: Offering Phoebe secretary work
jokes.punchline: Overshares about secretary's breast reduction surgery, then acknowledges the awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Awkward oversharing humor. The 'big boob story' line saves it from being just uncomfortable.

jokes.text: l don't know if it'd be your thing... because it involves being normal for a large portion of the day.
jokes.setup: Phoebe expressing interest in secretary job
jokes.punchline: Chandler points out the fundamental incompatibility between Phoebe and normalcy
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-based humor that's both affectionate and accurate about Phoebe's eccentricity.

jokes.text: ls it for dinosaur emergencies? Help. They're still extinct.
jokes.setup: Ross getting a beeper for paleontology work
jokes.punchline: Chandler mocks the idea of urgent dinosaur situations with fake emergency call
jokes.explanation: Clever absurdist take on Ross's job. The fake emergency call adds extra comedic layer.

jokes.text: All she has to do is dial 55-JlMBO.
jokes.setup: Explaining the beeper is for when Carol goes into labor
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous vanity number that spells JIMBO
jokes.explanation: Solid absurdist detail. Ross treating something so serious with such a silly number choice.

jokes.text: A cool number and a possible name for the kid.
jokes.setup: Ross's JIMBO beeper number
jokes.punchline: Chandler suggests it doubles as a potential baby name
jokes.explanation: Quick callback building on the absurdity of the JIMBO number. Efficient but not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: When we were....
jokes.setup: Monica saying Ethan is 'our age'
jokes.punchline: Her trailing off as she realizes how young that makes him sound
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing Monica's realization. Works through performance more than writing.

jokes.text: Rob that cradle.
jokes.setup: Learning Ethan is a college senior
jokes.punchline: Ross uses the cradle-robbing expression for age-gap relationships
jokes.explanation: Predictable use of common expression. Functional but lacks originality or character specificity.

jokes.text: Mature and grown-up, he's a big boy.
jokes.setup: Monica defending Ethan as mature and grown-up
jokes.punchline: Chandler sarcastically treats 'big boy' like it proves maturity when it emphasizes youth
jokes.explanation: Solid sarcastic response highlighting the contradiction in Monica's defense. Character-appropriate.

jokes.text: That'll stop when you pick up the phone.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asking if he can hear the phone ringing
jokes.punchline: Chandler states the obvious solution with deadpan delivery
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler deadpan pointing out the obvious. Simple but character-consistent.

jokes.text: l'm not in a meeting.
jokes.setup: Phoebe tells caller he's in a meeting
jokes.punchline: Chandler immediately contradicts her while she's still on the phone
jokes.explanation: Straightforward misunderstanding humor. Chandler's literal nature clashing with secretary protocols.

jokes.text: Most likely.
jokes.setup: Phoebe saying he actually has to get to work
jokes.punchline: Chandler's noncommittal response about doing his actual job
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler response - uncommitted and slightly sarcastic about basic responsibilities.

jokes.text: Did l turn the fish? Nope, because l made lasagna.
jokes.setup: Monica frantically preparing for her date
jokes.punchline: Her cooking anxiety making her check on fish that doesn't exist
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica character moment - her cooking obsession creating irrational anxiety. Self-correcting adds to the humor.

jokes.text: No, Andre is not here. Third time today.
jokes.setup: Ross gets beeped, thinks it's Carol in labor
jokes.punchline: It's actually people looking for Andre, a massage service
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection setup for the coming JUMBO/JIMBO confusion. The 'third time' adds frustration.

jokes.text: l do not perform those kind of services.
jokes.setup: Caller asking about massage services
jokes.punchline: Ross's horrified denial implies the services are sexual
jokes.explanation: Ross's prudish reaction to implied sexual services. Character-appropriate discomfort.

jokes.text: That's right. JUMBO with a 'U,' sir. You don't want me. Judging by his number, l'd be a huge disappointment.
jokes.setup: Directing caller to correct number (55-JUMBO instead of JIMBO)
jokes.punchline: Ross's self-deprecating joke about his own inadequacy compared to someone named JUMBO
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback with self-deprecating twist. Ross's sexual insecurity played for laughs with good wordplay.

jokes.text: They don't like you.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking why Phoebe didn't mention knowing him
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's blunt, matter-of-fact delivery of devastating news
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe directness delivering crushing news. Economy and character integration excellent.

jokes.text: Everyone. Except for.... No. Everyone.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking who doesn't like him
jokes.punchline: Phoebe starts to offer hope, then takes it away - it's literally everyone
jokes.explanation: Brutal escalation with perfect timing. The false hope makes the punchline even more devastating.

jokes.text: Mr. Bing. Boss Man Bing.
jokes.setup: Explaining how Chandler's promotion changed things
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous nicknames employees use, culminating in 'Boss Man Bing'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of ridiculous workplace nicknames. The alliteration in 'Boss Man Bing' is satisfying.

jokes.text: Could that report be any later?
jokes.setup: Phoebe saying coworkers 'do' Chandler impressions
jokes.punchline: Perfect imitation of Chandler's signature speech pattern and sarcasm
jokes.explanation: Iconic Chandler impression that became a defining trait. Perfect capture of his speech pattern and workplace sarcasm.

jokes.text: The hills are alive with the sound... of music. My scone. My scone.
jokes.setup: Continuing Chandler impressions
jokes.punchline: Absurd examples of Chandler applying his sarcastic delivery to random things
jokes.explanation: Good extension of the impression bit. The randomness of 'Sound of Music' and scone obsession adds absurdity.

jokes.text: That is so not-- That-- Oh, shut up.
jokes.setup: Hearing his own speech pattern mocked
jokes.punchline: Chandler starts to deny it but catches himself using the exact pattern
jokes.explanation: Perfect self-aware moment where Chandler proves the impression right. The self-correction is the joke.

jokes.text: Up until l was 9... l thought 'gunpoint' was a place where crimes happened.
jokes.setup: Joey sharing a childhood misconception
jokes.punchline: He literally thought 'gunpoint' was a geographic location
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Joey stupidity joke with perfect logic from a child's perspective. Original, character-perfect, and highly quotable.

jokes.text: Why do people continue to go there?
jokes.setup: Explaining his 'gunpoint' misconception
jokes.punchline: His logical confusion about why tourists would visit such a dangerous place
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the gunpoint joke. Joey's sincere concern about tourists adds another layer of absurdity.

jokes.text: lf that's what you kids are calling it these days, then... yes, l am.
jokes.setup: Monica asking if he's a virgin
jokes.punchline: Ethan treats 'virgin' like slang he's not familiar with
jokes.explanation: Good generational humor playing on age gap. The awkward formality contrasts with the intimate question.

jokes.text: l just haven't been to that particular one yet.
jokes.setup: Explaining he's excelled at every level
jokes.punchline: Treating sex like an academic level he hasn't reached yet
jokes.explanation: Clever academic metaphor for virginity. Fits character as college student and maintains dignity.

jokes.text: Even when you practice it at home, man, oh, man... it is nothing like that.
jokes.setup: Ethan describing his first sexual experience
jokes.punchline: Admission he practiced sex alone, implying masturbation
jokes.explanation: Awkward oversharing humor. Works through implication and Ethan's naive honesty.

jokes.text: You didn't mention the Tic Tac.
jokes.setup: Monica saying she knew she was attracted after their first kiss
jokes.punchline: Ethan reveals he had a Tic Tac, implying his kissing wasn't naturally good
jokes.explanation: Sweet callback that deflates the romantic moment with practical honesty. Good economy.

jokes.text: l just thought you were naturally minty.
jokes.setup: Ethan revealing the Tic Tac
jokes.punchline: Monica's innocent assumption about his natural mint flavor
jokes.explanation: Sweet response that maintains romantic innocence while acknowledging the practical reality.

jokes.text: l just had sex with someone who wasn't alive during the bicentennial.
jokes.setup: Learning Ethan is in high school
jokes.punchline: Monica's historical reference emphasizing the absurd age gap
jokes.explanation: Creative way to express age gap horror using specific historical reference. Highly quotable and original.

jokes.text: l just had sex.
jokes.setup: Monica's horror about the age gap
jokes.punchline: Ethan's simple joy about losing his virginity
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast between their perspectives. Economy and structure excellent - three words say everything.

jokes.text: My lie didn't make one of us a felon in 48 states.
jokes.setup: Ethan pointing out Monica also lied about her age
jokes.punchline: Monica's exaggerated legal consequences of their age gap
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with legal anxiety. The '48 states' specificity adds humor through false precision.

jokes.text: l'm Joan Collins.
jokes.setup: Comparing herself to women who date much younger men
jokes.punchline: Reference to actress known for dating younger men
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference that may not age well. Functional but relies on external knowledge.

jokes.text: Who?
jokes.setup: Monica's Joan Collins reference
jokes.punchline: Ethan's age showing through not knowing the reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect generational gap joke. One word that emphasizes their age difference and deflates Monica's reference.

jokes.text: There were times... when it wasn't even me.
jokes.setup: Ross asking about the arrangement in Monica's threesome dream
jokes.punchline: Monica reveals she was sometimes absent from her own sex dream
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd escalation. The idea of being absent from your own sex dream is wonderfully surreal.

jokes.text: This has nothing to do with him needing a note to get out of gym?
jokes.setup: Monica saying she's not seeing Ethan anymore
jokes.punchline: Chandler's reference to high school gym class excuse notes
jokes.explanation: Good callback highlighting the absurdity through specific high school detail. Classic Chandler snark.

jokes.text: Could you ask him which one the strongest Power Ranger is?
jokes.setup: Monica asking them not to mention Ethan's age
jokes.punchline: Ross treating him like a child with Power Rangers reference
jokes.explanation: Solid generational humor using specific kid's show reference. Less clever than previous age jokes.

jokes.text: lt's morphin' time.
jokes.setup: Power Rangers reference from Ross
jokes.punchline: Joey knows the catchphrase, showing he watches kids' shows too
jokes.explanation: Quick callback showing Joey's childlike interests. Character-appropriate but predictable.

jokes.text: That's part of the whole... them-not-liking-you extravaganza.
jokes.setup: Chandler surprised nobody told him about the office party
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's dramatic description of workplace exclusion as an 'extravaganza'
jokes.explanation: Good Phoebe vocabulary choice. 'Extravaganza' makes workplace exclusion sound festive and organized.

jokes.text: l don't wanna be the geek who invited the boss.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking to come to the party with her
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's blunt concern about her own social standing
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's typical self-interested honesty. The word 'geek' adds to the workplace social dynamics.

jokes.text: Then at four, Muppet Babies is on.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Ethan needing time to get over the breakup
jokes.punchline: Joey suggesting Ethan's schedule includes children's TV shows
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of treating Ethan like a child. Joey's own childlike interests make this character-appropriate.

jokes.text: You were great. But they still made fun of you.
jokes.setup: Chandler thinking the karaoke night went well
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's blunt assessment that success doesn't matter - they'll mock him anyway
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's crushing honesty about workplace politics. The 'but' makes it particularly brutal.

jokes.text: Mr. I'm-one-of-you Boss. Mr. l-wanna-be-your-buddy Boss Man Bing.
jokes.setup: Describing Chandler's various failed approaches
jokes.punchline: Increasingly desperate boss nicknames showing his failed attempts at connection
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of workplace nicknames showing Chandler's desperation to connect with employees.

jokes.text: lf she punches in 9-1-1... she's having a baby. Otherwise, l just ignore it.
jokes.setup: Explaining his new beeper system with Carol
jokes.punchline: Ross treating 911 as a casual communication method for labor
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff about Ross learning to filter beeper calls. The 911 casualness is absurd.

jokes.text: Frankly, it was a little more than l needed to know.
jokes.setup: Mentioning Andre got a call from cousin Nathan
jokes.punchline: Ross's prudish reaction to overhearing sexual details
jokes.explanation: Ross's typical discomfort with sexual topics. Character-consistent but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: l'm actually 30. l have a wife and a job. l'm your congressman.
jokes.setup: Ethan saying he's not 17 either
jokes.punchline: Absurd escalation to being a married congressman
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from age lies to completely absurd adult responsibilities. The congressman detail is unexpected and funny.

jokes.text: You're actually gonna throw this away because it's 'icky'?
jokes.setup: Monica using 'icky' to describe their relationship
jokes.punchline: Ethan's mature argument undermined by having to use the word 'icky'
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Ethan's mature argument and the juvenile word he has to quote. Shows their communication gap.

jokes.text: Moist.
jokes.setup: Asked if his hands are still wet
jokes.punchline: Chandler's uncomfortable word choice
jokes.explanation: Simple cringe humor. 'Moist' is an inherently uncomfortable word. Economical but basic.

jokes.text: Could these margaritas be any stronger?
jokes.setup: Chandler at the office party
jokes.punchline: Chandler using his own signature phrase pattern
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing Chandler unconsciously using his mocked speech pattern. Self-aware character humor.

jokes.text: Great. Now l'm having a baby.
jokes.setup: Ross arriving at what he thinks is the baby delivery
jokes.punchline: Ross's confused announcement that he's giving birth
jokes.explanation: Good confusion humor. Ross's panic making him claim impossible biological feat.

jokes.text: l'm hurt.
jokes.setup: Ross apparently getting physically injured during the panic
jokes.punchline: Understated announcement of injury amid baby chaos
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy beat. The understatement works but it's basic slapstick.

jokes.text: There are doctors there.
jokes.setup: Friends rushing Monica to leave for hospital
jokes.punchline: Monica's reason for wanting to look nice is to attract doctors
jokes.explanation: Good Monica character moment - her dating priorities even in crisis. Shows her desperation and vanity.

jokes.text: For the ride.
jokes.setup: Joey bringing food while they rush to hospital
jokes.punchline: His practical justification for bringing snacks to a birth
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - food is always practical. Character-consistent prioritizing of snacks over urgency.

tmdb_overview: Monica's stolen credit card leads to a thief hunt; Joey seeks a stage name change; Marcel reaches sexual maturity.
